description_long: This clock tower features a robust square brick base with a prominent white clock face on one side, adorned with black Roman numerals and black hands. Above the base, the structure transitions into a classical colonnaded section with arched openings on each side, supported by white columns and detailed cornices. The upper portion is capped by a greenish patinated dome, topped with a slender spire and finial. The architectural details include decorative moldings, pilasters, and a balustrade encircling the midsection. The clock face is set into the brickwork, framed by a white border, and is the focal point of the lower facade. The overall design is reminiscent of neoclassical civic architecture, suggesting a historic or municipal function.
description: A neoclassical clock tower with a brick base, white colonnaded midsection, and a green domed roof topped by a spire. The tower features a large Roman numeral clock face and ornate architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the clock face with Roman numerals, white columns, and the green dome above.
description_view_1: Rear view with no visible clock face, showing brickwork and arched openings beneath the dome.
description_view_2: Side view with the clock face partially visible, highlighting the colonnaded section and dome.
description_view_3: Alternate front/side view with the clock face fully visible and architectural details of the columns and dome.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is circular with a white background, black Roman numerals marking the hours, and two black hands for hours and minutes. The face is bordered by a simple white frame and lacks additional decorative elements.
